authoritative_sources.title: Threading Your Way Through the Labeling Requirements Under the Textile and Wool Acts
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC administers the Textile Fiber Products Identification Act and Wool Products Labeling Act, making this guidance authoritative for required apparel label content, dealer identity, and country-of-origin disclosure posture.

authoritative_sources.title: Clothes Captioning: Complying with the Care Labeling Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC enforces the Care Labeling Rule for textile wearing apparel and this business guide is authoritative for care-label instruction requirements and reasonable-care substantiation expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: Clothing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC is the product-safety regulator for consumer products in the U.S. and its clothing guidance is authoritative for apparel flammability, children's-product obligations, and safety-evidence boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: Tracking Labels on Children's Products
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC's tracking-label guidance is authoritative for the production-identification and traceability marks required on children's products, including children's apparel and footwear.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: production traceability and shipment documentation review
used_for: production run and carton traceability logic

authoritative_sources.title: Marking of Country of Origin on U.S. Imports
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CBP administers import marking requirements and this informed-compliance publication is authoritative for importer document expectations and country-of-origin marking posture for imported goods, including apparel.

authoritative_sources.title: Textile & Apparel Rules of Origin
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CBP's textile and apparel origin publication is authoritative for determining how origin is assigned for textile and apparel products and therefore what vendor, mill, and production evidence is required before origin-sensitive actions can be supported.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: origin determination and vendor evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: UFLPA Operational Guidance for Importers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CBP enforces the UFLPA and this operational guidance is authoritative for importer due-diligence, supply-chain mapping, and evidentiary expectations tied to forced-labor risk in textile and cotton supply chains.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: high-risk sourcing and forced-labor traceability review
used_for: mill, spinner, and source-document evidence expectations

authoritative_sources.title: Garment Industry Compliance Assistance Toolkit
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Labor Wage and Hour Division
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Wage and Hour Division enforces labor standards for the garment industry and its apparel-specific toolkit is authoritative for contractor labor-compliance expectations and domestic-apparel workflow boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: factory onboarding review and labor-risk escalation

authoritative_sources.title: SourcingStrong
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Labor Bureau of International Labor Affairs
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ILAB's SourcingStrong program is an official public labor-compliance tool tailored to apparel and footwear supply chains, making it authoritative for buyer-side due-diligence and worker-rights workflow expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: vendor due diligence and remediation workflow

authoritative_sources.title: Guide for Brands and Retailers Working with Manufacturers to Improve Working Conditions and Business Performance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Better Work is the ILO and IFC apparel-sector program and its public buyer guide is a recognized workflow reference for how brands use factory assessments, improvement plans, and follow-up with garment manufacturers.

authoritative_sources.title: Better Work Global Compliance Assessment Tool
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Better Work's public compliance assessment tool is a recognized apparel-factory framework for labor findings, indicator structure, and remediation tracking, which directly informs vendor-compliance evidence handling.

workflow_stages.name: request intake and apparel-scope triage
workflow_stages.description: Identify the style, SKU, colorway, season, age-grade, factory, program, country, shipment milestone, and requested decision before any vendor-compliance recommendation is prepared.

workflow_stages.name: control baseline and approval-gate mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map the style and factory to required labeling, care, flammability, children's-product, origin, UFLPA, and labor-compliance controls and name the exact evidence owners and review cadence.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: evidence assembly across product, labor, and origin records
workflow_stages.description: Assemble label approvals, lab reports, certificates, tracking details, audit findings, CAP status, origin worksheets, shipment documents, and vendor approvals needed to support a bounded answer.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: availability, shortage, and vendor-blocker handling
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether supply shortages, open CAPs, missing certificates, origin gaps, or unresolved factory status block replenishment or shipment-support work and identify the next owner.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: exception escalation and remediation packaging
workflow_stages.description: Translate missing evidence, policy-versus-practice drift, origin-risk cases, labor findings, and unauthorized approval requests into review-ready escalation notes without claiming customs, legal, product-safety, or financial authority.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: style, SKU, colorway, season, tech-pack, BOM, purchase-order, and vendor-calendar records
approved-factory matrices, subcontractor declarations, vendor scorecards, sourcing exceptions, and factory profile records
fiber-content, care-label, country-of-origin, RN, packaging, and artwork approvals
flammability test reports, GCC or CPC packets, children's-product tracking-label evidence, and lab-submission records
audit findings, Better Work or similar assessments, CAP trackers, remediation notes, and wage-hour or labor-risk records
commercial invoices, packing lists, carton-range logs, origin worksheets, UFLPA traceability records, and customs hold notices

allowed: classify apparel vendor-compliance requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
prepare evidence-backed availability summaries, vendor-risk notes, blocked-replenishment packages, and control summaries tied to named artifacts and owners
determine whether visible controls and evidence support bounded vendor-compliance handling without improvising spend, customs, product-safety, or legal authority
escalate: required tenant facts, style scope, age-grade, source-of-truth mappings, or approved-factory context are missing and materially change the answer
public-source, retailer-manual, jurisdiction, or tenant-policy conflicts materially change labeling, origin, labor, or shipment posture
open CAPs, missing certificates, origin-traceability breaks, or unresolved labor findings increase vendor or shipment risk
requests for customs interpretation, legal interpretation, product-safety signoff, final shipment release, or factory approval
refuse: requests for final legal signoff, customs-origin rulings, product-safety approval, shipment release approval, or purchase-order approval
requests to proceed with sourcing, substitution, or shipment when required label, certification, origin, or labor evidence is missing or contradictory
requests that collapse the lane into generic vendor management and ignore apparel-specific style, age-grade, factory, origin, and labeling realities

domain_failure_modes: Allowing goods to ship because the vendor is trusted even though current care-label, fiber-content, or children's-product evidence is incomplete.
Treating country-of-origin and UFLPA documentation as optional import paperwork instead of a shipment-blocking evidence chain.
Approving a factory or mill substitution as routine replenishment even though sourcing, customs, product-compliance, or buyer approvals are unresolved.
Treating labor-audit pass status or a generic scorecard as equivalent to current CAP closure and worker-rights remediation evidence.
Producing a vendor-compliance conclusion without style, factory, age-grade, and shipment context tied to named systems of record.
Treating the apparel label as cosmetic and answering from generic vendor-management language rather than style, label, origin, and factory controls.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Required supply is unavailable or short
Vendor or substitution decision exceeds documented authority
Traceability or inventory evidence is incomplete
A required control is missing evidence
Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Supplier or vendor issue that blocks execution
Attempt to bypass approval or release controls

unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources establish the core U.S. baseline, but retailer RSL programs, Prop 65 overlays, and private vendor manuals differ by tenant and must be retrieved at runtime instead of guessed.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: jurisdiction-specific forced-labor and customs escalation triggers outside the U.S. baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: This pack uses a U.S.-facing public baseline because the lane issue did not pin a non-U.S. trade regime; cross-border deployments must retrieve the applicable jurisdiction-specific customs and forced-labor overlay.
